summary: Session established the core architectural diagnosis for HiveMind revamp. The central problem is not only stale role prompts but fragmented authority: there is a technical runtime entry but no hard behavioral entry, no single source of truth robust to stop/resume, and prompt surfaces are louder than runtime state. The agreed direction is: many user-facing entry variants, one runtime-owned intake kernel, one canonical intake/source-of-truth authority, and AGENTS/agent markdown as projections rather than the real SOT. Runtime bootstrap was completed through hivemind_runtime_command hm-init, not manual file writes.
nextSteps: 1) Start fresh session from this handoff. 2) Treat runtime as already attached and profile-complete. 3) First implementation cycle: update AGENTS.md
and mirrored .opencode/agents/*.md so orchestrator never executes directly and all agents have explicit may_execute/may_delegate/terminal/gatekeeping contracts. 4) After AGENTS-first persistence

define the canonical entry-kernel + intake-record + route-order + resume/reattach model. 5) Only then proceed to hm-plan / deeper architecture work for runtime-owned intake and AGENTS deep-init adaptation.

packet.scope: Restart handoff for fresh session after runtime attachment bootstrap and architecture diagnosis.
constraints: Treat commands as prompt contracts only. Deterministic routing must live in plugins/tools/hooks. Shell is non-interactive/no-TTY. Do not bootstrap HiveMind by manual file writes. First persisted implementation priority is AGENTS governance and role-boundary projection before broader architecture work.
memoryScope: Preserve this handoff as the canonical resume brief for the next fresh session. Use it instead of relying on prior chat memory.
successMetrics: 1) New session recognizes runtime attachment is already bootstrapped. 2) Next work starts from AGENTS-first governance persistence. 3) Planning assumes many entry variants but one runtime-owned intake kernel and one canonical SOT. 4) No direct implementation begins before role/governance surfaces are addressed.
packet.returnContract: On resume, summarize bootstrap state, restate AGENTS-first mandate, then continue with governance persistence before any other implementation.
packet.resumeTarget: Use this handoff as the first context artifact in the new session after restart.
